Title: IWS - Verify IR frame renders in Web UI
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network and accessible via IP address.

Title: 2. Open the device Web UI in a web browser.
3. Navigate to the detection zone / live view page.
Preconditions: 1. Observe the IR frame view area.

Title: 2. Verify the live thermal image is displayed within the circular lens boundary (red border indicates physical FOV edge).
3. Wait for the auto-refresh cycle to complete.
4. Verify the frame updates.
Preconditions: 1. The IR frame renders a live thermal image of the sensor's field of view.

Title: 2. The circular lens boundary is clearly visible (red area outside the FOV).
3. The frame refreshes automatically without manual intervention.
4. No rendering artifacts or blank frames appear.
Preconditions: Manual
Steps: Web UI > IR Frame / Detection Zones

Title: IWS - Verify color palette switching in Web UI
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network and accessible via IP address.

Title: 2. Open the device Web UI in a web browser.
3. Navigate to the detection zone / live view page.
4. The IR frame is rendering with the default color palette.
Preconditions: 1. Click the color palette dropdown.

Title: 2. Select the rainbow/jet palette. Verify the IR frame updates to use the new colors.
3. Select the hot/yellow palette. Verify the IR frame updates.
4. Select the grayscale palette. Verify the IR frame updates.
5. Select the blue-to-red (cool-warm) palette. Verify the IR frame updates.
Preconditions: 1. Each palette option renders the IR frame with distinct, correct color mapping.

Title: 2. Switching palettes updates the view immediately (on next frame refresh).
3. Thermal intensity gradients are clearly distinguishable in each palette.
4. No rendering errors or fallback to a default palette on switch.
Preconditions: Manual
Steps: Web UI > IR Frame / Detection Zones

Title: IWS - Verify color palette selection persists on page reload
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network and accessible via IP address.

Title: 2. Open the device Web UI in a web browser.
3. Navigate to the detection zone / live view page.
Preconditions: 1. Select a non-default color palette (e.g., grayscale).

Title: 2. Verify the IR frame renders with the selected palette.
3. Refresh the browser page (F5).
4. Observe the color palette selection and IR frame rendering.
Preconditions: 1. After page reload, the previously selected color palette is still active.

Title: 2. The IR frame renders with the persisted palette, not the default.
3. The dropdown shows the correct palette as selected.
Preconditions: Manual
Steps: Web UI > IR Frame / Detection Zones

Title: IWS - Verify layout persistence for multi-sensor group
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are on the network, grouped together in the Web UI.

Title: 2. Open the Web UI for the sensor group.
3. A spatial layout (arrangement of sensors relative to each other) has been configured.
Preconditions: 1. Document the current multi-sensor layout arrangement.

Title: 2. Close the browser and reopen the Web UI for the sensor group.
3. Verify the layout is the same as documented.
4. Power cycle one of the sensors in the group.
5. Reopen the Web UI for the sensor group and verify the layout.
Preconditions: 1. The multi-sensor layout persists after closing and reopening the Web UI.

Title: 2. The spatial arrangement of sensors is identical to what was configured.
3. Power cycling a single sensor does not affect the group layout.
4. No sensors are displaced or missing from the layout view.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify sensor grouping logic in Web UI
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are on the network and accessible.

Title: 2. Open the device Web UI in a web browser.
Preconditions: 1. Create a new group containing two or more sensors.

Title: 2. Verify all selected sensors appear in the group.
3. Remove one sensor from the group.
4. Verify the removed sensor is no longer part of the group.
5. Add the sensor back to the group.
6. Verify the sensor reappears in the group.
Preconditions: 1. Sensors can be grouped together in the Web UI.

Title: 2. The group reflects the correct membership after add/remove operations.
3. Grouped sensors share a combined coverage view.
4. Ungrouped sensors operate independently.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify grouped sensors show combined zone coverage
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped together in the Web UI.

Title: 2. Detection zones are configured on each sensor.
Preconditions: 1. Open the group view in the Web UI.

Title: 2. Verify that each sensor's detection zone is visible in the combined layout.
3. Verify that overlapping coverage areas between sensors are represented correctly.
Preconditions: 1. All grouped sensors' detection zones are visible in a single combined view.

Title: 2. The spatial relationship between sensors is accurately represented.
3. Overlapping zones are clearly identifiable.
4. Person counts from the group reflect the combined coverage.
Preconditions: Manual
